title: Pedilonum victoriae-reginae
intro: Pedilonum victoriae-reginae, also known as Victoria's pedilon, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Africa. The health benefits of this plant include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating various conditions such as skin infections, wounds, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Pedilonum victoriae-reginae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Pedilonum victoriae-reginae is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Australia and is often found in the tropical regions of the country. The classification of Pedilonum victoriae-reginae places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Gentianales, and family Rubiaceae. The plant is also known by its synonym, Psychotria victoriae-reginae. Further classification may be necessary to determine the specific relationships within the Rubiaceae family.

Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever and skin conditions.
